Contention Resolution Schemes (CRSs) have emerged as a general framework for obtaining solutions to constrained combinatorial optimization problems. They involve obtaining a fractional solution to a continuous relaxation of the objective and then rounding it to an integral one.

Online Contention Resolution Schemes (OCRSs) are used when the order of rounding is controlled by an adversary. They have been successfully applied in settings such as prophet inequalities and stochastic probing.

An offline adversary, which is the weakest of the three, has to fix an ordering of the elements before any of the elements are revealed.
An almighty adversary, the most powerful one, has access to the realizations of all random events.
In between the two extremes is the online adversary. An online adversary’s choices can only depend on the realizations of the elements that have appeared so far.
We also define the notion of a greedy OCRS, which provide guarantees with respect to an almighty adversary.
